items.title: Darwin mum's killer ordered to spend indefinite period in custody

children.content: Sheena Fairfield was stabbed to death outside her family home more than three years ago. Her killer will now spend 20 years in supervised custody.

gallery.altText: A court sketch of a white man, with auburn curly / wavy long hair, scruffed, looking right, blue shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A court sketch of a white man, with auburn curly / wavy long hair, scruffed, looking right, blue shirt.

items.title: Alleged murderer's bail in limbo as Tiwi Islands town erupts in riots

children.content: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

image.altText: A photo from a plane shows a remote community sitting on the edge of an island, part of the aircraft's wing is visible.

items.title: Government sues 3M Australia over PFAS concerns at 28 Defence bases

children.content: The Commonwealth is suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence bases across Australia.

image.altText: Fire fighter sprays foam on a fire

items.title: The surprising city claiming to be Australia's street art capital

children.content: Artists say Darwin has surpassed Melbourne as Australia's street art capital
with work underway on a fresh round of murals across the Top End city.

gallery.altText: A close up painting of a woman's face in purple, pink, blue and black painted on a building viewed through black fence palings
A wall is painted in colourful patterns, with a blurred person walking in the background
A painting of a red tailed black cockatoo on the side of a tall yellow building

items.title: Legal action filed over decision to clear almost 3,000ha of tropical savanna

children.content: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Murray Watt frowns.

items.title: NT child protection bill one year in the making had 'limited' consultation

children.content: Despite taking a year to prepare a controversial bill surrounding child protection, the NT Department of Children and Families says consultation with organisations was "limited".

image.altText: Front-on shot of white man, pale blue shirt, and bright blue striped tie, sitting at long white desk, inside high-rise office

items.title: Police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody still not known

children.content: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

articles.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death

children.content: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has concluded.

image.altText: A group of Aboriginal mourners walking through a coles supermarket, while holding branches.

gallery.altText: A woman bends over in mourning next to a pile of flowers laid on the ground.

thumbnail.altText: A woman bends over in mourning next to a pile of flowers laid on the ground.

items.title: Feds warn NT's law-and-order agenda could breach human rights

children.content: Federal Attorney-General Michelle Rowland warns her Northern Territory counterpart that the NT's tough-on-crime agenda could breach Australia's international human rights obligations.

image.altText: Rowland stands in the prime minister's courtyard smiling.

items.title: Brother of hit-run victim says family will appeal NT court's 'light' sentence

children.content: The family of a man who was left to die alongside his severely injured brother and father after a hit-and-run crash, say they will appeal the "light" sentence handed to the man who was behind the wheel.

image.altText: A coat of arms on a building, alongside the words: Supreme Court.

items.title: NT paramedics consider body-worn cameras amid ongoing violent assaults

children.content: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

gallery.altText: Two paramedics in uniform pose with an ambulance

thumbnail.altText: Two paramedics in uniform pose with an ambulance

items.title: 'Scary as hell': Divers on the upcoming mission to rescue Laos villagers

children.content: A diver describes the "emotional moment" when he found a group of men alive after they were trapped for more than a week in a Laos cave, while a South Australian renowned for his ability to navigate tight spaces joins the rescue mission.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit and orange helmet climbing backwards in a cave.
A man in a full body wet suit and orange helmet wading through chest-deep water in a dark cave.
A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.

items.title: ABC appoints Reuters exec Simon Robinson as news director

children.content: Senior Reuters executive Simon Robinson takes over from former director Justin Stevens who announced his resignation yesterday after four years in the job.

image.altText: A man with greyish hair poses for a photo.

items.title: Young police officer's death raises questions about mental health support

children.content: An inquest into the death of a NSW Police recruit who was less than three months into the job hears differing opinions about whether it was related to his work.

image.altText: Hands on a black jacket with a NSW Police patch

items.title: Blues hunting Women's Origin clean sweep on soaked Gold Coast

children.content: The Women's State of Origin series wraps up on the Gold Coast, with NSW hunting a clean sweep at a rain-soaked Robina Stadium on the Gold Coast. Follow live.

image.altText: Rugby league player running with the ball, tackled around the chest, holding another defender by their jersey

items.title: Hawks in control at Docklands after minute's silence for Daniher

children.content: The Hawks are looking to consolidate a spot in the top four as they take on St Kilda at Docklands, on a day when details of Neale Daniher's state funeral are confirmed. Follow live.

image.altText: A man stands with a microphone in front of a podium with flowers and guernseys, and the big screen says "Vale Neale Daniher".

items.title: Meet the Iranians who gather every night outside Khamenei's house

children.content: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

items.title: Greens signal willingness to help Labor push capital gains changes through in weeks

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced the bill to parliament today, which the government hopes to pass before the winter recess.

image.altText: The treasurer glances at the camera to his right as he sits in parliaments among other frontbenchers.

items.title: NRL backs referee's decision to send off Ponga in Origin I

children.content: The NRL supports Ashley Klein's controversial decision to send off Kalyn Ponga, saying the State of Origin referee got the call right.

image.altText: Kalyn Ponga sent off by Ashley Klein in State of Oriign I.

items.title: Shopping habits to help reduce your grocery spend

children.content: Small changes to the way we approach our meal planning can make a big difference to the overall cost.

items.title: Why this photo has been terrifying the internet for years

children.content: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

image.altText: A empty yellow room

items.title: The most influential female composers in history and today

children.content: From Hildegard von Bingen to Hildur Guðnadóttir, here are some of the most influential women of the music world in history and today.

image.altText: Italian composer Francesca Caccini seated and playing a lite with back turned.

items.title: Drone with $1 million worth of contraband intercepted at Townsville prison

children.content: Queensland Corrective Services say the drone was carrying illicit drugs, syringes and a mobile phone.

items.title: Family 'burst into tears' after Lauren Huxley attacker denied parole

children.content: Robert Black Farmer was handed a 24-year sentence over the attempted murder of Lauren Huxley at her Northmead home in 2005.

image.altText: Sydney TAFE student Lauren Huxley

items.title: Four Corners reporter Mahmood Fazal sacked by ABC

children.content: Mahmood Fazal has been sacked by the ABC following an internal investigation into an appearance on a podcast hosted by a former underworld figure, managing director Hugh Marks says.

image.altText: A man with a full black beard.

items.title: Woman charged with joining IS remanded in custody

children.content: Police conducted searches at homes in Melbourne's north early on Thursday as part of ongoing investigations into people who have returned from Syria.

image.altText: A woman in black Islamic garb is flanked by two uniformed police officers.

items.title: IS-linked mother back in childhood bedroom in Sydney's south-west

children.content: The father of one of the returned Islamic State group-linked women says his daughter is "happy to be home" but exhausted after more than a decade stranded in Syria.

image.altText: Nesrine Zahab with her veil lifted

items.title: Mother whose two children died suddenly calls for genetic testing

children.content: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

gallery.altText: A mother with blonde hair and a white dress stands in front of the ocean holding a young baby girl in a white outfit.

thumbnail.altText: A mother with blonde hair and a white dress stands in front of the ocean holding a young baby girl in a white outfit.

items.title: Ex-police officer admits sharing bed but denies rape, court hears

children.content: A former South Australian police officer says he got into bed with a woman while she was sleeping and caressed her thigh soon after she rejected his advances, but denies raping her, a court has heard.

image.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

items.title: No new evidence in Gus Lamont search, but police 'won't give up'

children.content: A renewed search for missing four-year-old boy Gus Lamont has failed to uncover additional evidence, police reveal.

image.altText: An SA Police officer at Oak Park Station.

items.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate in coming decades

children.content: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

image.altText: The third will collected by the Office of the Public Trustee Queensland.

items.title: Wheel falls off Link Airways flight during take-off from Canberra Airport

children.content: Damage to bearing parts meant investigators could not determine what caused a wheel to separate from a Link Airways-operated flight departing Canberra for Newcastle.

image.altText: A plane's wheels, with one wheel missing.

items.title: US Justice Department launches criminal probe into E Jean Carroll

children.content: The probe is focused on whether Carroll committed perjury in testimony tied to two civil lawsuits that she won against Mr Trump — one tied to ‌her allegations that he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

gallery.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: Greens push for minister's resignation after legal fee revelation

children.content: Madeleine Ogilvie is being accused of deliberately misleading parliament, after she revealed her involvement in a Supreme Court matter, months after saying she was not connected to any legal action.

image.altText: A woman sits in a green chair in parliament

items.title: Bolivian president warns country at 'breaking point' after weeks of protests

children.content: The protests have caused shortages of food, fuel and life-saving medicine and members of Bolivia's Indigenous community have called for the president's resignation.

image.altText: A small fire in front of several police officers on motorbikes or standing.
